aliases: Chicken McCrispy® (1pc) Spicy Upsized Meal *Parts may vary

english: Chicken McCrispy® (1pc) Spicy Upsized Meal *Parts may vary

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0019 represents the main component (fried chicken) as a direct code for a complete fried chicken meal with fries and beverage is not available in FoodEx2; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 227490003 represents the main component (fried chicken) as a direct code for a complete fried chicken meal with fries and beverage is not available in SNOMED CT; closest_parent used.

serving.description: One meal (1 piece spicy fried chicken, large fries, medium soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving consists of one piece of spicy fried chicken, a large portion of french fries, and a medium carbonated soft drink, totaling approximately 670 grams.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo (Composite Estimation)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are estimated by combining data for individual components (fried chicken, french fries, soft drink) from the HPB Singapore Foodo database, based on a typical meal composition of 120g chicken, 150g large fries, and 400g medium soft drink.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database (Analogous Estimation)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index is estimated as a composite of its main components. French fries (McDonald's) have a GI of 75 (high) and Coca-Cola has a GI of 63 (medium) per University of Sydney GI Database. Fried chicken itself has a lower GI, but the overall meal's GI is weighted towards the higher carbohydrate components, resulting in a medium GI estimate of 65.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption due to the high sodium content (approximately 820 mg per serving), which can contribute to elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate and sugar content, high glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Due to its high carbohydrate and sugar content and a significant glycemic load of approximately 67, this meal can cause rapid spikes in blood glucose levels, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat and sodium content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high saturated fat (approximately 12 g per serving) and sodium levels in this meal can negatively impact cardiovascular health, increasing the risk of heart disease and related complications.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy McCrispy Chicken Meal from McDonald's Singapore is a popular fast-food offering, featuring a piece of juicy fried chicken coated in a spicy, extra crispy batter, served alongside a large portion of french fries and a medium carbonated soft drink. The chicken is typically deep-fried to achieve its characteristic crunch and bold spicy flavor. This meal is known for its satisfying, savory, and spicy taste profile, with the fries providing a salty, starchy contrast and the soft drink offering a sweet, refreshing balance. Nutritionally, it is a high-calorie, high-carbohydrate, high-fat, and high-sodium meal, primarily due to the fried components and sugary beverage. It is considered an ultra-processed food. From a clinical perspective, its high glycemic load and significant levels of saturated fat and sodium make it a food to be consumed sparingly, especially for individuals managing conditions like Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The meal is certified halal and is widely available at McDonald's outlets across Singapore.
common_user_queries: Spicy fried chicken and fries meal

confidence_reason: Nutrient and glycemic index values are estimated based on a composite of individual components (fried chicken, french fries, soft drink) from approved sources, as a direct entry for the exact branded meal was not found. The meal composition is based on typical McDonald's serving sizes.
notes: Nutrient values for monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as they were not reported in the composite estimation from HPB Singapore Foodo for the individual components. Ontology: FoodEx2 match_type set to closest_parent as no exact FoodEx2 code exists for a complete fried chicken meal with fries and beverage; code A0019 (chicken, fried) is used as the closest parent. SNOMED CT match_type set to closest_parent as no exact SNOMED CT code exists for a complete fried chicken meal; code 227490003 (Fried chicken) is used as the closest parent.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Populated all mandatory fields; applied HPB Foodo composite nutrient estimation; applied University of Sydney GI analogous estimation; ensured multilingual naming compliance; populated ontology with FoodOn, FoodEx2, SNOMED CT; developed LLM-ready description; provided health context for all three required conditions; ensured alias consistency.

Iteration 3: Fixed g5_ontology_honesty failure by changing foodex2_match_type from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' and snomed_ct_match_type from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent', conforming to the allowed set ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none']. Updated gap notes accordingly.
